task_completion.description: Analyze the historical Apache access log to find the top IPs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing commands executed to analyze /home/ga/historical_access.log (e.g., using combinations of awk, sort, uniq -c, or head) and displaying the top IP addresses and their request counts.

task_completion.description: Create a report at /home/ga/blocked_ips.txt listing the top three IP addresses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output (like cat or echo) showing the creation and content of /home/ga/blocked_ips.txt containing exactly 3 IP addresses.

task_completion.description: Ensure the report uses the exact format IP_ADDRESS : REQUEST_COUNT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of /home/ga/blocked_ips.txt strictly follow the requested format with a colon separating the IP and the count (e.g., '192.168.1.50 : 842').

task_completion.description: Update the Apache configuration to block the three identified IP addresses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano or vim) or terminal command showing modifications to an Apache configuration file (e.g., /etc/apache2/apache2.conf, /etc/apache2/sites-available/000-default.conf, or a .htaccess file) adding access control directives to block the 3 IPs (e.g., 'Require not ip <IP>' or 'Deny from <IP>').

task_completion.description: Ensure the Apache configuration changes are active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a successfully executed command to restart or reload the Apache service (e.g., 'sudo systemctl restart apache2', 'sudo service apache2 reload', or 'sudo apachectl restart').

integrity.description: The agent must not access the privileged ground truth file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no commands reading, opening, or extracting data from /tmp/ground_truth.json.

integrity.description: The reported IPs and counts must come from actual log analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The IP addresses and request counts written to /home/ga/blocked_ips.txt should match the output visible in the terminal when the agent analyzed /home/ga/historical_access.log.

integrity.description: The Apache configuration must be genuinely modified.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual file editing interfaces (nano/vim) or valid shell redirection commands (echo ... >> config) rather than just printing text to the console.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The exact target IPs and their request counts are randomized per run. The top 3 IPs will have between 400 and 950 requests each, while the remaining noise IPs will have 5-45 requests. The VLM should verify that the IPs the agent blocks and reports match the highest counts seen in the agent's own terminal output during the log analysis step.
